title: podman remote clients for windows and mac links are broken in readme file
body: podman-remote latest clients links which are part of readme are broken and there is no way to download those clients from the mentioned urls.

comments.body: We've started adding the binaries to the individual Github releases (e.g. the [v1.9.1](https://github.com/containers/libpod/releases/tag/v1.9.1) release). We should update the readme to reflect that.
@mheon thanks but it doesn't have for linux binary, any plan to add that or that is complete depend on distribution way of providing?
@praveenkumar given that the linux client is not truely static (distributions will need different binaries), how would you recommend we do this (if we could)?
If we make a static binary, it should only be podman-remote not full podman.

> If we make a static binary, it should only be podman-remote not full podman.

@baude @rhatdan Yes, I am right now only interested in the podman-remote binary not full podman since on the release page we are only listing the remote clients and those are static binary if I am not mistaken?

Another thing I notice here on https://github.com/containers/libpod/releases/tag/v1.9.1 page there is one zip file which name as podman-remote for windows and then there is msi for windows  but it is just named as podman, shouldn't that would also podman-remote ? Also there is no tarball of podman-remote for macOS/Linux on the release page.
No, that was a special request but a agroup we work with.

Podman exutables on MAC and Windows, should be called Podman NOT podman-remote.  We should only use/install the `podman-remote` executable on Linux boxes, to eliminate confusion with the full featured `podman` command.

I installed "podman-remote" in a special PATH (as "podman"), so that I can troubleshoot podman-remote issues also on Linux... The alternative would have been to just call it `podman-remote` everywhere.
Is there any place can find the tarball file of podman for macOS?

> Is there any place can find the tarball file of podman for macOS?

I think it is available in Homebrew ? `brew cask install podman`

> 
> 
> > Is there any place can find the tarball file of podman for macOS?
> 
> I think it is available in Homebrew ? `brew cask install podman`

The Homebrew version stays at 1.6.1-dev, I hope to find a newer version.

They should be available from here.
https://github.com/containers/libpod/releases/tag/v1.9.2

The mac binary seems to be missing yet, maybe it is upcoming ?
(there is windows and static and .msi, but no darwin or mac installer)

Was there any volunteers for adding podman to homebrew proper ?
(to build it from source code, rather than the current binary "cask")

I'm not sure if you are missing all that much by going with 1.6 ?

It's only the client, most of the improvements happen server-side
Thanks, I will try the homebrew version.
@mheon Why is there no mac client on https://github.com/containers/libpod/releases/tag/v1.9.2?
Nobody told me to build one, so I didn't - I assume we do want one?
We want a MAC Version yes, primarily for CRC.
Fixed now
Link for Windows is still broken on https://podman.io/getting-started/installation
Sorry about that, I fixed it.  I accidently marked a pre-release as a release.
